symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden, tearing, maximal-at-onset chest pain radiating to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden tearing/ripping maximal-at-onset front-to-back pain is the most specific historical feature of dissection.
Severe chest pain overlaps with ACS, but ACS pain is classically crushing/pressure radiating to jaw/arm rather than tearing to the back.

symptoms.symptom_name: Interscapular back radiation, pain unaffected by movement/respiration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Interscapular radiation tracks the descending aorta; non-pleuritic, non-positional pain argues against MSK and PE.
MSK pain is typically movement/position dependent.
Signals haemodynamic compromise (tamponade, rupture, or acute severe AR) — a marker of severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marfanoid phenotype (tall stature, arachnodactyly, long limbs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marfan syndrome with aortic-root dilatation is among the strongest risk factors for dissection in a young adult.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior ectopia lentis (dislocated eye lens by history)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ectopia lentis is a cardinal Marfan feature, corroborating the connective-tissue substrate.
Untreated aortic-root dilatation loses the protective dP/dt reduction of beta-blockade, raising dissection risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inter-arm systolic BP differential >20 mmHg (right > left)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Branch-vessel involvement produces asymmetric perfusion; a clinician-measured sign, sealed — the AI Doctor infers severity from history, not a measured differential.
Pulse deficit from dissection extending into the left subclavian territory — palpable only in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: New early-diastolic murmur of aortic regurgitation with widened pulse pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Proximal (Type A) extension disrupting the aortic valve; audible only on auscultation, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT aortogram showing intimal flap / aortic root dilatation

investigation_details.investigation_name: CT aortogram (contrast CT of the aorta)
investigation_details.expected_result: Intimal dissection flap involving the ascending aorta (Stanford Type A) with aortic-root dilatation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Dissection undiagnosed and untreated — high risk of rupture and death.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cross-sectional imaging is the diagnostic gold standard — only available in hospital, never via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tearing pain + inter-arm BP differential/pulse deficit + new aortic-regurgitation murmur is the classic in-person triad; in telehealth only the first is obtainable, so the history plus the Marfan substrate must carry the recognition.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency CT aortogram (in hospital, after 000 transport)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis and surgical planning for suspected Type A dissection — arranged by the receiving hospital, not by the AI Doctor.
Assesses aortic root, valve regurgitation and pericardial effusion when CT is delayed — hospital-based.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ECG and troponin (to assess for coronary involvement)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Type A dissection can involve the coronary ostia; also helps distinguish primary ACS — but must NOT delay imaging or transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home/self-directed repeated observations or waiting for a GP appointment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Any remote work-up or delay is dangerous; the only correct action is immediate 000 escalation.
